;THIS PROGRAM IS USED TO TEST ATTINY2313 //THIS PROGRAM IS USED TO TEST ATTINY2313
; LED IS CONNECTED TO PORT PORTB.0 ;THROUGH 100 //LED IS CONNECTED TO PORT PORTB.0 //T
;OHM THE LED WILL FLASH ;EVERY ONE SECOND //HROUGH 100 OHM THE LED WILL FLASH EVERY ONE
SECOND
i
.device at90s2313 ; Device is AT90S2313 k
.include "TN313def.inc" include <tiny231a3.h>
#define LED bPORTB.0
.def temp = r16 void delay()l;
e
.def dly= r17
d
.def delcnt= r18 ; Loop Counter void main(void)
b
DELAY: clr delcnt ;Init Loop Counter {
loop1: dec delcnt APORTB = 0x00;
nop DDRB = 0xFF;
m
brne loop1
dec dly while (1)
a
brne loop1 {
s
ret LED = 1;
s
delay();
o
RESET: ldi temp, LOW(RAMEND) LED = 0;
H
out SPL, temp ; Initialize SPL delay();
sertemp }
.
out DDRB ,temp ; PORTB = all routputs }
out PORTB, temp
D
loop: cbi PORTB, PB0 void delay()
{
ldi dly, $FF ; Initialize delay of about 65 ms unsigned int x,y;
rcall DELAY ; Number of cycles = 1026 * del + 4 for(x = 0; x<1000; x++)
sbi PORTB, PB0 for(y = 0; y<100; y++);
ldi dly, $FF ; Initialize delay of about 65 ms }
rcall DELAY ; Number of cycles = 1026 * del + 4
rjmp loop ; Repeat foreverAfter assembling flasher.asm , or compiling flasher.c, the machine
language file flasher.hex is produced as shown.
